body.title: agent_datacenter: skeleton MCP aggregator — single endpoint, namespace routing
body.result: Skeleton in agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py. BaseDevice + FastMCP. rack.devices/rack.health/rack.channels tools. register_device/deregister_device. Collision detection (RegistrationError). Per-device .health MCP tool added on registration. All tests pass.
body.description:
The skeleton is the single MCP endpoint everything connects to. It namespaces and proxies tool calls to the appropriate device.

Problem: Without a central MCP aggregator, CC and Igor must know about each device's MCP server separately. The aggregator gives one unified address space.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py:
  class Skeleton(BaseDevice):
    Uses MCP SDK (mcp or anthropic-mcp package; check latest)
    MCP server with dynamic tool registration
    Registers itself as device #1: 'skeleton'
    Tool namespace routing:
      tools prefixed 'rack.*' handled internally (rack.devices, rack.health, rack.channels)
      tools prefixed '{device_id}.*' proxied to that device's registered MCP endpoint
    register_device(device_id, mcp_endpoint) — adds tool prefix entry
    deregister_device(device_id) — removes tool prefix entry (keeps mailbox)
    Device registration creates MCP tool namespace entry
    Namespace collision: if device_id already has tools registered → raise RegistrationError before starting device

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: Skeleton extends BaseDevice (implements full device contract itself)
- no-sqlite: no SQLite; state in flat-file registry only

Scope boundary:
IN: MCP aggregator, namespace routing, rack.* tools, proxy to device endpoints
OUT: IMAP mailbox creation on registration (T-adc-imap-mailbox-lifecycle), health rollup implementation (T-adc-rack-health-rollup)

Test plan:
- Skeleton starts; rack.devices() returns [skeleton]
- Register stub device → its tools appear under {device_id}.* namespace
- Call {device_id}.health → proxied correctly
- Deregister device → its tools disappear from namespace
